notes: Elicia's editor-loop verbs for Pomegranate Hour. The listener loop (RECORD / TRANSCRIBE / REWIND / PLAY BACK) is now the editor loop — running the master timeline, scrubbing, committing cuts. Buyable pool is the location-bound work: CALL IN (phone), PATCH (patch bay routes you into an episode), SHELVE (library archives a master), SCROLL (library plays you a take from another session). THE LONG QUIET is Elicia's signature — she waits the way only she can wait. Second stat is DOUBT (rises bad); Insight is the spendable currency — episodes earned by listening.

cards.title: RECORD
cards.flavor: Commit the take. The master reel turns. Whatever they're saying is now somewhere on the timeline; the timeline doesn't lie about what happened.

cards.title: TRANSCRIBE
cards.flavor: Cut the take down to what it's actually saying. Costs insight; the sentence comes loose from the noise around it and the visitor hears themselves in a shape that wasn't there before.

cards.title: REWIND
cards.flavor: Scrub back to the moment you almost missed. The timeline runs in both directions when she runs it.

cards.title: PLAY BACK
cards.flavor: Play the assembly back to the room. Some visitors only finish their thought after they hear it once at -18 db on the monitor wall, with Davey's cue underneath.

cards.title: CALL IN
cards.flavor: Pick up the phone. Marisol's car is forty minutes out. Hen's third call goes through if you make it. Reggie always picks up.

cards.title: PATCH
cards.flavor: Route a cable into an episode. The suite hears the footage. The footage hears the suite. For a turn, an episode-space and the booth become one space — the subjects can be addressed without leaving the room.

cards.title: SHELVE
cards.flavor: Take a finished master from the timeline and shelve it. The suite is one cut lighter; tonight has one more episode worth of room. The label goes on in your hand.

cards.title: THE LONG QUIET
cards.flavor: Stop the room. The eighty-six-minute clock keeps running but the people in it do not. Patience does not tick for one full turn. Elicia waits the way only she can wait, the way that made her an editor in the first place.

cards.title: VEIL
cards.flavor: Pull the curtain across the control glass. The visitor in the booth hears nothing but themselves at -18 db. Their next two beats land in one move.

cards.title: SCROLL
cards.flavor: Pull a DAT from the wall and play one cue from another night. Davey's hand. Young Elicia's hand. Hen's voicemail from '02. The suite remembers; sometimes the suite remembers things you'd rather it didn't.
